attractors.frame: The dangerous transaction is the conversation.
attractors.why_high_impact: Shifts control from downstream sequence checks to the real interception point where multi-turn bio misuse knowledge is assembled.

attractors.frame: BioGuard is a portable conversation-layer protocol; BKT is the scoring contract.
attractors.why_high_impact: Replaces ad-hoc model tuning with a reusable interface that operators and integrators can adopt across hosts.
Provides immediate operational value through decision + uncertainty + rationale, not just offline threat probabilities.

attractors.frame: Prototype evidence should expose the next protocol revisions.
attractors.why_high_impact: Keeps the claim bounded while making failures useful for follow-up work.

attractors.frame: BioGuard gives operators an auditable checkpoint with replay, not just a score.
attractors.why_high_impact: Makes the project demoable, legible to judges, and directly useful for bio-LLM interface operators.

attractors.frame: Protocol portability is measured through conformance artifacts, not by host claims.
attractors.why_high_impact: Turns portability from a demo property into a reproducible, reviewable science object.

acceptance: Decision and schema fields match required payload.
Escalation rules are deterministic and documented.

responsibilities: Implement BKT heuristics and decision envelope output.
Support operator replay and audit flow.
acceptance: 500+ multi-turn fixtures are preferred when throughput permits; lower counts must be justified with tier balance.
decision envelope schema fields are complete for 200/400/422 cases.
Operator replay path is present and demoable.

responsibilities: Build baseline matrix for at least one keyword and one model baseline.
Emit compact result table and bootstrap summary.
Produce tier breakdown and ablation comparison.
acceptance: One primary comparison table with all baselines.
CI and significance labels are populated.

responsibilities: Map artifacts to the reproducibility path.
acceptance: Abstract and method sections are readable without repo-specific jargon.
Dual-use appendix present with ethical and disclosure lines.
Portability and operator-utility frames are explicit.

milestones.name: Contract Lock
milestones.goal: Freeze scoring contract, output formats, and public evidence boundary.

milestones.name: Core Runtime
milestones.goal: Reference runtime, skill pack, and operator replay flow runnable end-to-end.

milestones.name: Evidence
milestones.goal: Produce primary table, ablation table, and portability notes.

milestones.name: Public Submission
milestones.goal: Deliver polished workshop-style paper, public repo surface, and reproducibility path.
